purpose: Define fair library and framework baselines for block-topk-sampling before claiming custom HIP wins.

seed_contract.sortedness: top-k candidates sorted by descending logit
seed_contract.tie_policy: lower token id wins equal logits
seed_contract.rng_policy: precomputed uniform per row is consumed by the kernel; RNG generation is not included
seed_contract.probability_policy: softmax(top_k_logits / temperature) over the selected top-k candidates
seed_contract.top_p_policy: not implemented by the seed task
seed_contract.nan_policy: not part of the seed contract

result_classifications.meaning: Custom kernel is correct and faster for the same contract.
Custom kernel wins by exploiting documented fixed shape, fusion, layout, RNG, or omitted generality.
Performance is close enough to be useful, with no correctness gap.
Best matching library or serving path is faster.
End-to-end serving path wins even if an isolated custom kernel is faster.
Attempt is correct but slower, and the reason is useful corpus data.
Sortedness, tie policy, RNG, top-k/top-p semantics, processors, or output materialization differ.
Baseline path, workspace, RNG, processor, or launch boundary is missing.
Baseline or custom path does not build.
Guidance or scaffold only; no GPU measurement is attached.

workload_distinctions.definition: Keep candidates by rank, then renormalize probabilities over the selected k candidates.
must_record: whether top-k candidate list is materialized
whether sampling uses logits or probabilities

workload_distinctions.definition: Keep the smallest probability-ordered prefix whose cumulative probability reaches p.

baselines.sortedness: unsorted output only in the current API
baselines.tie_policy: not guaranteed for equal elements across the kth boundary
baselines.workspace_allocation: requires temp storage query and device-accessible temporary storage
baselines.seed_contract_status: contract-mismatch unless sortedness and deterministic tie repair are added or the custom contract is changed to unsorted
notes: Record cuda::execution determinism and output_ordering requirements.
Do not compare unsorted non-deterministic top-k directly against the sorted seed output.

baselines.sortedness: preserves original relative order among selected items; does not rank selected items
baselines.tie_policy: inherited from threshold or predicate producer
baselines.logits_processors: not included unless fused into predicate producer outside DeviceSelect
baselines.workspace_allocation: requires temp storage query and device-accessible temporary storage
baselines.seed_contract_status: not a standalone equivalent baseline for top-k sampling
notes: Use only as one stage of a fully timed pipeline.
Record the separate threshold computation and launch count.

covers: sorted top-k by taking first k after full sort
deterministic oracle when composite keys encode tie policy
baselines.sortedness: sorted descending when descending API is used
baselines.tie_policy: stable sort behavior; seed tie policy requires composite key or post-processing
baselines.workspace_allocation: requires temp storage; API variants use approximately N or 2N auxiliary storage
baselines.seed_contract_status: equivalent for sorted top-k only if tie policy is encoded and sampling/softmax is added
notes: A full sort may be a strong correctness oracle but a weak performance baseline for fixed small k.
Record whether double-buffer or non-double-buffer APIs are used.

covers: finished sequence and runtime argument handling
baselines.sortedness: path-dependent; record whether selected candidates are returned, sorted, or only sampled
baselines.tie_policy: implementation-dependent; vLLM on ROCm documentation does not guarantee a specific tied-probability policy
baselines.rng_inclusion: uses library RNG state for sampling paths; record CURAND state and whether RNG is timed
baselines.logits_processors: serving path may include temperature, penalties, logits processor hooks, return logits/logprobs, and batching policy
baselines.workspace_allocation: record top-k, top-p, or AIR top-p workspace helpers and reuse/allocation policy
baselines.seed_contract_status: equivalent only for top-k temperature sampling when processors, RNG, output materialization, and timing boundary are matched
notes: Do not compare seed top-k-only sampling to top-p, beam, or return-logprob behavior as if they were equivalent.
Record sampler backend: Torch Sampler, TRTLLM Sampler, or direct kernel invocation.

baselines.sortedness: path-dependent; record actual selected path and output contract
baselines.tie_policy: path-dependent; PyTorch, Triton, FlashInfer, and CUDA paths may differ
baselines.rng_inclusion: path-dependent; record PyTorch generators, FlashInfer deterministic mode, or fallback random_sample behavior
baselines.logits_processors: record whether vLLM built-in or custom logits processors ran before sampling
baselines.workspace_allocation: record framework allocations, contiguous copies, and any library workspace
baselines.seed_contract_status: equivalent only if the selected vLLM path matches top-k temperature sampling, RNG, output materialization, and processor boundary
notes: Record the actual dispatch path selected by vLLM.
Per-request generators or processed logits/logprobs can force fallback paths.

baselines.sortedness: sampling APIs may not materialize sorted candidate lists; record exact outputs
baselines.tie_policy: statistical sampling contract; exact tied-token behavior is path-dependent
baselines.rng_inclusion: seed and offset arrays or scalar values; record deterministic mode and whether RNG is timed
baselines.logits_processors: not included unless applied before FlashInfer call
baselines.workspace_allocation: record dynamic shared memory and external workspace for AIR/top-p/renorm paths
baselines.seed_contract_status: equivalent only when logits/probability input, RNG policy, and materialized outputs match the seed contract
notes: If sampling is statistically equivalent but exact sampled ids differ, classify as near-match only with distribution tests.
Include softmax timing when using probability-consuming APIs unless the custom path also starts from probabilities.

baselines.sortedness: sorted if torch.topk(sorted=True) is used; unsorted if sorted=False
baselines.tie_policy: PyTorch topk tie behavior may not match lower-token-id ties unless repaired
baselines.rng_inclusion: torch.multinomial generates RNG internally; record generator and synchronization behavior
baselines.logits_processors: not included unless separately applied in framework code
baselines.workspace_allocation: record tensor allocation, allocator warmup, output preallocation, and framework dispatch
baselines.seed_contract_status: good oracle but not necessarily strongest performance baseline; exact seed RNG policy requires custom use of precomputed uniforms instead of torch.multinomial
notes: Use this baseline to check sorted output and probability values.
Do not ignore CPU-GPU synchronization or allocator effects.

must_not_claim: top-p equivalence from a top-k-only seed
fair serving comparison unless logits processors, RNG, workspace, and launch boundaries are recorded
sorted top-k equivalence when the baseline returns unsorted candidates
exact sampled-token equivalence when the baseline only promises statistical equivalence
